person.name: Robert IV de Courcy - Baron de Courcy

nameForms.fullText: Robert IV de Courcy - Baron de Courcy

km.Courcy, Calvados, Normandy, France: កូuរស៊ី, ខាលវ៉ាដុស, ណូរម៉ានឌិe, ប្រទេសបារាំង

zh.Courcy, Calvados, Normandy, France: 法國諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯庫希
zh.lifeSummary: Robert IV de Courcy - Baron de Courcy於1186年出生在法國諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯庫希。他和Mathilde de Houdetot至少有1個兒子。他去世於1239年在他的家鄉享年53歲。

zh-hans.Courcy, Calvados, Normandy, France: 法国諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯庫希
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Robert IV de Courcy - Baron de Courcy于1186年出生在法国諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯庫希。他和Mathilde de Houdetot至少有1个儿子。他去世于1239年在他的家乡享年53岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Courcy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphonse, Andree, Jacques, Michel, Monique.
French: habitational name from any of the places in Calvados, Loiret, Manche, and Marne called Courcy.
History: Nicolas Lemière dit Courcy from Courcy in Manche, France, married Louise-Geneviève Dubé in Rivière-Ouelle, QC, in 1749.

nameMeanings.name: Baron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French, English, Dutch, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and Croatian; Breton (also Le Baron): from Old French barun, baron, Middle English barun, baroun, Middle Dutch baroen, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and dialectal Croatian baron ‘baron’ (i.e. a nobleman ranking below an earl and above a knight), a title of nobility which was in England also awarded to certain freemen of the cities of London and York and of the Cinque Ports. As a surname it is unlikely to be a status name denoting a person of rank. Generally, it referred to service in a baronial household or was acquired as a nickname by a peasant who had ideas above his station or applied to a man showing characteristics of a nobleman. See also 3 and 4 below, compare Barron and Lebaron .
French: habitational name from (Le) Baron, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare   Lebaron  .
German: status name for a freeman or baron, from barūn ‘imperial or church official’, a loanword in Middle High German from Old French (see 1 above).

variants.name: Barron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Miguel, Raul, Pedro, Ruben, Alfredo, Luis.

Scottish: status name from Scots baron, which in the Highlands denoted a small proprietary landholder. In Angus it alluded specifically to a tenant of one of the small baronies attached to the Abbey of Coupar Angus. The surname was taken by Scottish migrants to Ulster. Compare Baron .
Scottish: shortened form of McBarron, from Scottish Gaelic Mac a' Bharain ‘son of the baron’. See Baron and 1 above.

variants.name: Boron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Krystyna, Edyta, Leszek, Mieczyslaw, Mieczyslawa, Wladyslaw.
Polish (Boroń): from a pet form of the personal names Borzysław or Borzymir, formed with the element bor ‘to fight’ (from Old Slavic boriti se).
French: habitational name from a place so called in Franche-Comté.

variants.name: Baro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Mario, Manuel, Ana, Angel, Carlos, Cesar, Fernando, Jesus, Jose, Orlando, Ramon, Rosalia.  Italian Guido, Angelo, Carmelo, Federico, Lucio.
Catalan (Baró): status name from baró ‘free man, baron’ (see Baron 1), or a habitational name from a place so named.
Hungarian (Báró): status name from báró ‘baron’. In the form Baró, it could be from a pet form of the personal names Barabás (see Barabas ) or Bertalan.

variants.name: Barona
meanings: Spanish: from the feminine form of Barón ‘baron’ (see Baron ), applied as a nickname or as an occupational name for a member of the household of a baroness.
Italian: probably from the feminine form of   Barone  . The name is concentrated in Sondrio province (Lombardy).

variants.name: Barren
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Baron   or   Barran  .

English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

French: habitational name from any of the places in Calvados, Loiret, Manche, and Marne called Courcy.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Vivian   and   Vivien .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: Robert IV de Courcy - Baron de Courcy was born in 1186, in Courcy, Calvados, Normandy, France. He had at least 1 son with Mathilde de Houdetot. He died in 1239, in his hometown, at the age of 53.

fr.lifeSummary: Robert IV de Courcy - Baron de Courcy est né en 1186, à Courcy, Calvados, Normandie, France. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Mathilde de Houdetot. Il est décédé en 1239, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 53 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Robert IV de Courcy - Baron de Courcy wurde 1186 in Courcy, Calvados, Normandie, Frankreich geboren. Er hatte mit Mathilde de Houdetot mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1239 im Alter von 53 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Robert IV de Courcy - Baron de Courcy è nato il nel 1186 nel Courcy, Calvados, Normandie, Francia. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Mathilde de Houdetot. Morì nel 1239, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 53 anni.

ja.Courcy, Calvados, Normandy, France: フランスノーマンディカルヴァドスコウルシ
ja.lifeSummary: Robert IV de Courcy - Baron de Courcyは1186年にフランスノーマンディカルヴァドスコウルシで生まれた。彼はMathilde de Houdetotとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1239年に故郷で53歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert IV de Courcy - Baron de Courcy(은)는 1186년 프랑스 노먼디 캘바도스 쿠르시에서 태어났다. 그는 Mathilde de Houdetot(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1239년 그의 고향에서 53세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Robert IV de Courcy - Baron de Courcy nasceu em 1186, em Courcy, Calvados, Normandie, França. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Mathilde de Houdetot. Ele faleceu em 1239, em sua cidade natal, com 53 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Robert IV de Courcy - Baron de Courcy родился в 1186 г. в местности коурси, калвадос, норманди, Франция. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Mathilde de Houdetot. Он умер в 1239 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 53 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Robert IV de Courcy - Baron de Courcy nació en 1186, en Courcy, Calvados, Normandie, Francia. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Mathilde de Houdetot. Murió en 1239, en su pueblo, a la edad de 53 años.

th.Courcy, Calvados, Normandy, France: คูรซิ, คัลแวโดส, เนอรแมนดี, ฝรั่งเศส
